businessBackground: Topicus.com is a European acquirer, manager, and builder of vertical market software (VMS) businesses, spun out of Constellation Software (TSX: CSU) in January 2021. The company operates across 40+ niche verticals in both public and private sectors, providing mission-critical, industry-specific software solutions. CSU retains 30.4% economic ownership and 50.1% voting control via a super voting share. Topicus Coop is the principal subsidiary (64.18% owned by Topicus).
catalysts: ~~Feb 25, 2026~~: ~~FY2025 annual FS + MD&A filed on SEDAR+~~
~Apr-May 2026: Asseco Poland FY2025 annual report (WSE) — Partial read-out 2026-04-17: FY2025 revenue PLN 16.78B (+11...
May 5, 2026: TOI Q1 2026 press release (after close) — hard-dated 2026-04-15
May 15, 2026: CSU AGM — joint hybrid CSU+TOI+Lumine; joint Q&A 9:00 AM ET; pre-submit question form

blindspots: **Asseco subsidiary-level performance** — TOI owns ~23% of Asseco but Asseco has 30+ subsidiaries across CEE/Israel. No visibility into which subsidiaries drive value or how CSU playbook is being applied. Monitor via: Asseco annual report (Apr-May).
**Acquisition IRR by vintage** — TOI doesn't disclose returns by acquisition cohort. Aggregate FCFA2S growth is the proxy. If FCFA2S growth decelerates despite high M&A pace, IRRs may be declining.
**AI adoption depth across 180+ BUs** — 27-61% of BUs "using AI" but no disclosure on revenue impact or cost savings. Monitor via: management commentary in annual FS.
**Competitive dynamics in European VMS** — Fragmented market with limited public data on win rates or pricing pressure. Proxy: organic growth stability.

cyclical.context: Should trend up with SaaS mix. >75% = positive; <68% = quality concern
cyclical.name: Recurring revenue %

cyclical.context: Revenue in EUR; stock priced in CAD. FX translation affects reported multiples
cyclical.name: EUR/CAD exchange rate

cyclical.context: Multiple compression driven by rate environment + acquisition sentiment. Tracks rate expectations
cyclical.name: EV/FCFA2S multiple

noiseFilter: **Quarterly FCFA2S variability** — Acquisitions create lumpiness. Judge on rolling 4-quarter or annual basis only.
**Individual acquisition announcements** — TOI does 40-50 acquisitions/year. No single deal is thesis-altering. Track aggregate pace and quality.
**CSU share price moves** — TOI is operationally independent. CSU sentiment affects TOI stock but not TOI fundamentals.
**Netherlands revenue concentration commentary** — 44.4% is known. Gradual diversification expected. Don't react to country-level revenue shifts <5pp.

structural.name: FCFA2S growth (YoY)
structural.threshold: <10% for 2 consecutive years = compounding thesis broken; >20% = bull case confirmed

structural.name: EBITDA margin
structural.threshold: <23% = acquisition dilution concern; >28% = operating leverage thesis validated

structural.current: EUR 1,039M FV vs EUR 524M cost (2x). Van Poelje absent from CSU calls — CRITICAL GAP
structural.name: Asseco relationship health
structural.threshold: Any public friction, write-down, or forced divestiture = thesis impairment. CSU AGM May 15 = first read

structural.current: **~1.0–1.2x.** Net debt €742.4M minus Asseco investment (€524M cost / €489.9M carrying) = €218–253M operating net debt / €218.7M FCFA2S. Within thesis.
structural.name: **Operating** leverage (net debt ex-Asseco / FCFA2S)

structural.current: **3.4x (EUR 742.4M net debt).** ⚠️ **Above prior >3.0x threshold.** Driven by €384.9M net Asseco investment + €390M VMS acquisitions. Net debt/CFO = 1.8x. FY2025 CFO €412.7M. **Revised interpretation:** Asseco is strategic equity investment (mark-to-market €1,039M); the debt-like 3.4x figure misrepresents operational risk. Revised total-leverage thresholds: >3.5x = operational concern, >4.0x = balance-sheet risk. **Resolution: CHANGELOG MINOR (see Themes/AI Disruption/CHANGELOG.md 2026-04-17).**
structural.name: **Total** leverage (incl Asseco) (net debt / FCFA2S)
structural.source: TOI FY2025 annual FS; CHANGELOG resolution 2026-04-17

structural.name: M&A deployment pace
structural.threshold: <$200M annual = growth engine stalling; pipeline visibility is leading indicator of future FCFA2S

trendLog.action: No action — monitor CSU AGM May 15
trendLog.assessment: All indicators on track. FCFA2S +23%, organic 4% stable, leverage elevated but manageable at 1.67x. Asseco relationship is key unknown.

trendLog.action: **CHANGELOG evaluation required.** Recommend: (1) verify €742.4M vs THESIS.md figure, (2) consider bifurcating threshold into "operating leverage" (ex-Asseco) and "total leverage" (incl Asseco), (3) assess whether 3.4x constrains further M&A pace or signals increased risk. CSU AGM May 15 remains highest-priority catalyst.
trendLog.assessment: FY2025 annual FS (reported Feb 25) shows EUR 742.4M net debt. Increase driven by: EUR 384.9M net Asseco investment + EUR 390.4M VMS acquisitions = ~EUR 662M total deployed in FY2025. Net debt/CFO = 1.8x (CFO €412.7M) — less alarming on cash flow basis. FCFA2S +23% provides deleveraging runway. **Key question: is Asseco (strategic equity stake in publicly traded company) fundamentally different from typical VMS bolt-on debt?** Asseco FV €1,039M vs €524M cost = 2x marks. If Asseco marked-to-market, "economic" leverage much lower. However, the debt is real and the threshold is defined. Prior figure (€365.9M) was likely from interim period or excluded Asseco financing.
trendLog.new: **3.4x (EUR 742.4M net debt).** ⚠️ **BREACH of >3.0x threshold** ("balance sheet risk").

trendLog.action: No action on FCFA2S/organic — on track. Leverage breach requires separate CHANGELOG evaluation (above).
trendLog.assessment: FCFA2S +23% confirms >20% bull case. Organic 4% stable (8 quarters). Q4 FCFA2S +40% is exceptional. Net income decline is non-operational (Asseco accounting). EBITDA margin not explicitly updated — prior 25.5%.

trendLog.action: **CHANGELOG MINOR written (Themes/AI Disruption/CHANGELOG.md 2026-04-17).** No thesis revision. No conviction rescore.
trendLog.assessment: Apr 6 breach resolved via methodology refinement: the 3.0x threshold conflated operating M&A debt with strategic minority-equity-investment financing. Bifurcated into operating leverage (ex-Asseco) and total leverage (incl Asseco) with distinct thresholds. Asseco stake carries a €1,039M fair-value mark vs €524M cost (2x); the "debt" funding it is economically offset by a marketable security. V4 (Financial Health) scoring unchanged. Q1 2026 (May 5) will provide first mid-year FCFA2S + leverage trajectory read; CSU AGM (May 15) Q&A may elicit van Poelje on leverage trajectory target.
trendLog.new: 5a Operating ~1.0–1.2x (OK); 5b Total 3.4x (revised threshold >3.5x operational / >4.0x BS-risk; OK)

trendLog.action: **CHANGELOG MINOR.** No thesis revision. Indicator #4 (Asseco relationship health) — **slightly strengthened, still ⚠ Unknown** pending May 15 AGM van Poelje commentary.
trendLog.assessment: PEMS is the strategic frame justifying Topicus's Asseco deal — architect-level continuity at the moment Topicus executes its first major PEMS-style deployment. Governance impact on TOI is structurally nil (Leonard not on TOI board). Scenario probability shift <2pp (bull +1pp, bear -1pp); below 5pp CHANGELOG-MAJOR threshold.
trendLog.new: Mark Leonard not standing for CSU board re-election post May 15 AGM; continues as **PEMS advisor**.
